diagnoses.name: Alcoholic cirrhosis of liver with ascites
diagnoses.evidence_span: The patient has a significant medical history including alcoholic cirrhosis of the liver with ascites

diagnoses.name: Secondary esophageal varices without bleeding
diagnoses.evidence_span: He has documented secondary esophageal varices without active bleeding but at risk for hemorrhage

diagnoses.name: Portal hypertension
diagnoses.evidence_span: The patient carries several chronic diagnoses including alcoholic cirrhosis with associated portal hypertension

diagnoses.name: Portal vein thrombosis
diagnoses.evidence_span: Portal vein thrombosis was identified on prior imaging

diagnoses.name: Hepatitis C without hepatic coma
diagnoses.evidence_span: Additionally, the patient has known hepatitis C infection without hepatic coma

diagnoses.name: Severe protein-calorie malnutrition
diagnoses.evidence_span: the patient demonstrates signs of severe protein-calorie malnutrition given his liver disease and poor intake

diagnoses.name: Intestinal adhesions with obstruction
diagnoses.evidence_span: Imaging and clinical presentation were consistent with intestinal obstruction secondary to adhesive bands, likely from prior infection

diagnoses.name: Nicotine dependence, cigarettes
diagnoses.evidence_span: He reports long-standing nicotine dependence with cigarette use

diagnoses.name: Ascites
diagnoses.evidence_span: which complicated his clinical course during this admission

procedures.name: Imaging (to evaluate bowel obstruction)
procedures.evidence_span: Imaging and clinical presentation were consistent with intestinal obstruction secondary to adhesive bands

medications.name: Sodium chloride (isotonic/0.9%)
medications.evidence_span: IV fluids including iso-osmotic sodium chloride and 5% dextrose solutions were administered

medications.name: Dextrose 5%
medications.evidence_span: IV fluids including iso-osmotic sodium chloride and 5% dextrose solutions were administered

medications.name: Potassium chloride
medications.evidence_span: Electrolyte repletion was provided with potassium chloride 40 mEq

medications.name: Magnesium sulfate
medications.evidence_span: and magnesium sulfate (both 2 gm and 4 gm doses) via IV route

medications.name: Heparin
medications.evidence_span: Heparin 5000 units was given subcutaneously for thromboembolism prophylaxis

medications.name: Pantoprazole
medications.evidence_span: Gastrointestinal support included pantoprazole and famotidine for acid suppression

medications.name: Famotidine
medications.evidence_span: Gastrointestinal support included pantoprazole and famotidine for acid suppression

medications.name: Bisacodyl
medications.evidence_span: bisacodyl (both 10 mg PO and 10 mg PR) for bowel motility

medications.name: Furosemide
medications.evidence_span: Diuresis was managed with furosemide 20 mg IV given the ascites

medications.name: Oxycodone
medications.frequency: every 6 hours as needed for pain
medications.evidence_span: Pain control was achieved with oxycodone 10 mg PO/NG

medications.name: Hydromorphone
medications.evidence_span: and hydromorphone 0.25-0.5 mg IV titrated as needed for comfort
